title: Protesters march demanding justice for victims of violence in Manipur
content: Valte, who had sustained injuries in a mob attack during the early period of ethnic violence in 2023, died on February 20 in a private hospital in Gurugram.

title: Use cooking fuel judiciously, consumers told
content: Hoteliers in Tamil Nadu fear distributors may cite shortages to divert supplies to the black market or increase prices, raising operating costs for restaurants and hotels

title: Having conversations on AI’s role in Wikipedia, says founder Jimmy Wales
content: Jimmy Wales says AI can contribute by making translations better but warns that “even a good machine translation” can fall short of expectations if “cultural questions” are not addressed

title: Domestic LPG price hiked by ₹60 amid West Asia conflict
content: Non-subsidised LPG, the one that common household users other than the Ujjwala beneficiaries use in their kitchens, will now cost ₹913 per 14.2 kg cylinder in Delhi, according to the Indian Oil Corporation (IOC) website; the price increase is effective from March 7

title: 204 of 238 Indian cities did not meet air quality standards: CREA
content: Ghaziabad was the most polluted city, recording an average PM2.5 concentration of 172 g/m³, followed by Noida (166 g/m³), and Delhi (163 g/m³), as per CREA, a Finland-based independent research group.

title: In a Bengal college, an Iftar party to be held by women for women
content: The event, called ‘2026 Dosti ki Iftar’ and being organised by the Deeniyat Muallima College (located in Santragachi, across the river from Kolkata) and the city-based initiative Know Your Neighbour, intends to “provide a space for women to exchanges their views, and break myths and stereotypes”.

title: Fadnavis presents ₹7.69 lakh crore Maharashtra Budget, loan waiver for farmers
content: The Budget proposes a ninefold growth of the agricultural economy, aiming to raise the sector’s GDP from $55 billion to $500 billion by 2047. The State’s revenue receipts are estimated at around ₹6.56 lakh crore, resulting in a revenue deficit of about ₹40,500 crore.

title: Andhra Pradesh firecracker unit blast: Gone in a flash fire
content: An explosion at a firecracker manufacturing unit in Vetlapalem village of Kakinada district, Andhra Pradesh, claimed 28 workers’ lives, including eight women. Preliminary investigations reveal that the unit’s owners had allegedly stored explosive materials far beyond the permitted limit and engaged 31 workers, nearly four times the number allowed under licence, in a bid to fulfil festival and wedding orders, reports T. Appala Naidu

title: Why does India require U.S. nod for securing its energy needs, ask Opposition leaders
content: The Opposition leaders question the government’s silence over U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent’s latest remark objecting to the tone of his announcement ‘allowing’ India a temporary waiver to purchase Russian oil

title: U.S. waiver to procure Russian oil: Kerala flays Centre
content: It is outrageous that a country like India should appear to require approval from the U.S. for decisions such as oil imports, says Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan in a post on X

title: Youth, student groups criticise Karnataka Budget over jobs, education funding
content: Student and youth organisations have criticised the State Budget for 2026-27, alleging that it fails to address the pressing issues of unemployment among youth and the declining state of public education

title: YSRCP shirking responsibility of raising public issues in Assembly, says A.P. CM
content: The ₹3.32 lakh-crore budget has been approved in the Assembly, but a concerted effort is needed to spend every single rupee judiciously, says Naidu

title: Woman in Assam becomes first to get Indian citizenship after two years in detention camp
content: Dipali Das, who entered India in February 1988, was declared a non-citizen by the Foreigners’ Tribunal in Silchar

title: 56 Naan Mudhalvan scheme trainees clear civil services exam
content: All India Civil Services Coaching Centre, the institute of the State government, has registered an increase in results owing to Naan Mudhalvan Scheme.

title: Regulating internet is Centre’s domain, social media ban imposed by A.P., Karnataka may hit jurisdictional barrier
content: Recently, the Union Minister for Electronics and Information Technology Ashwini Vaishnaw said that the government was discussing age-based restrictions on social media use

title: Budget has increased debt burden, say BJP and JD (S)
content: In his pursuit of records,  Siddaramaiah has pushed the State’s economy in disarray: Union Minister Kumaraswamy

title: Pinarayi slams Centre’s ‘submission’ before US over strikes on Iran
content: The country being tied to imperial powers is a matter of concern, and strong public opinion should arise against it, says Chief Minister

title: Watch: Iranian warship strike near Sri Lanka raises questions for India
content: An Iranian warship was struck by a U.S. submarine near Sri Lanka, days after participating in India’s MILAN 2026 naval exercise. The incident raises major questions about India’s role in the Indian Ocean and the strategic balance between Washington and Tehran.

title: West Asia conflict: Airlines bring back 15,000 stranded passengers
content: As many as 51 flights are planned on Saturday (March 7, 2026)— the highest since airstrikes in the region beganon February 28, 2026

title: Budget reflects State’s continued focus on diverse growth: trade bodies
content: FKCCI welcomes several important initiatives aimed at strengthening the industrial ecosystem and creating new opportunities for investment, innovation, and employment

title: Iran vessel with 183 sailors docked in Kochi on March 4
content: IRIS Lavan has been docked at Kochi since March 4 as the vessel had developed technical issues. “Its crew of 183 are currently accommodated at naval facilities in Kochi,” the source said

title: India must ask U.S. why it is targeting Iranian ships in Indian Ocean: Iran Minister Khatibzadeh
content: Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh says his country is a “responsible power” and has not shut the Strait of Hormuz, he says the attack on Iran is “based on a lie” and pitches for diplomacy as the “only option”

title: Proposal to rename Victoria Hospital in Bengaluru takes doctors, patients by surprise
content: Doctors at the hospital say they had no prior information about the proposal until it was announced in the Budget

title: Karnataka Budget 2026–27: Capex slows as committed expenses balloon and revenue takes a hit
content: Karnataka pins hopes on raising its own tax revenue and increasing borrowing to push capital expenditure

title: A.P. liquor scam: ED attaches properties worth ₹441 crore of key accused
content: Assets belonging to Kesireddy Rajasekhara Reddy. Booneti Chanakya and Donthireddy Vasudeva Reddy attached under PMLA as probe points to alleged ₹3,500 crore loss to State exchequer during previous regime

title: SIR was conducted in a transparent manner, says CEC
content: Gyanesh Kumar quoted as saying in an official release that the objective of the SIR was to ensure that no eligible elector was left out and that no ineligible person was included in the electoral rolls

title: ₹2,000 crore electric bus programme with World Bank aid announced in Karnataka Budget 2026
content: The government also plans to induct 4,000 electric buses into the fleets of various State-run transport corporations as part of the initiative

title: Karnataka’s GSDP expected to grow at 8.10% against India’s GDP growth of 7.4%
content: While Bengaluru Urban has the highest per capita income at ₹8.55 lakh, Kalaburagi has the lowest with ₹1.44 lakh.

title: Trapped in toxicity: Residents of Visakhapatnam’s Gajuwaka struggle for breath of fresh air
content: The Garbage Transfer Station (GTS) located in Ward 76 of Gajuwaka amid densely populated colonies, has become a source of severe pollution and health issues. Locals complain of constant foul odour, black coal dust, mosquito menace, respiratory and skin ailments. Despite repeated agitations, the residents fear that long-term health consequences are imminent due to official apathy, explains Harish Gilai

title: Lok Sabha likely to debate resolution seeking Speaker Om Birla’s removal on March 9
content: Both the BJP and the Congress have issued whips mandating their members’ presence in the House from March 9 to 11, in anticipation of the resolution by 118 Opposition MPs in the Lok Sabha seeking Birla’s removal for partisan behaviour slated for debate

title: New Intermediate Ring Road connecting Tumakuru and Mysuru Roads, pedestrian push in Budget for Bengaluru
content: The State govt. has also allowed the new corporations to mobilise resources for development works through ‘municipal bonds’ based on their balance sheets

title: Nishant Kumar to join JD(U) on March 8, as Nitish promises to stick around
content: Nishant Kumar was not present at the emotional meeting of JD(U) leaders who offered him their “wholehearted” support; RJD’s sole RS candidate Amarendra Dhari Singh claims he has the votes to win

title: BRS faults Congress for giving full-page ads in Kerala with public money
content: Former Minister Harish Rao accuses Congress of funding a misleading poll campaign in Kerala with promises it failed to fulfil in Telangana

title: Karnataka Budget 2026: Aerospace common facility, flight training school to be set up
content: Siddaramaiah also announced that ₹200 crore has been earmarked for the development of seven domestic airports in the State

title: Watch: The Namakkal case that changed Indian elections forever
content: Can courts intervene when an election is already underway? In 1952, a rejected candidate from Namakkal took this question to the Supreme Court in the landmark N.P. Ponnuswami v. Returning Officer case.

title: New bodies for upliftment of deprived communities
content: In the year 2026-27, a total of ₹44,632 crore has been provided under Karnataka Scheduled Castes Sub-Plan (SCSP) and Tribal Sub-Plan (TSP)

title: Man gets life imprisonment in murder case
content: The accused beat the victim to death and took away Rs.750 from his pocket, said GRP CI J.V. Ramana, who investigated the case

title: CPI(M) flags anomalies in voter rolls, seeks clarity from election commission
content: M.V. Jayarajan cites the discrepancy between the voter rolls prepared by the State Election Commission and the final roll published by the Election Commission of India following the SIR to highlight the party’s concerns

title: I&B Ministry orders halt to TRP reporting of TV news channels for four weeks
content: The order has been issued in view of the “unwarranted sensationalism” in reporting the ongoing war in West Asia involving the U.S., Israel and Iran

title: Kerala shines in Civil Services exam; over 10 candidates in top 200
content: Sreeja J.S. from Naruvamoodu in Thiruvananthapuram secures 57th rank in her  first attempt, overcoming several oddsTHIRUVANANTHAPURAM

title: 9,000 posts in health sector filled in 20 months, says A.P. Minister
content: On an average, 450 vacancies have been filled every month under the government’s ‘zero vacancy’ policy aimed at strengthening healthcare facilities, says Satya Kumar Yadav

title: Mamata’s dharna in Kolkata is an attempt to disrupt SIR: West Bengal BJP
content: The BJP says that the Mamata Banerjee is abdicating her constitutional responsibility by conducting a dharna against the Election Commission of India

title: Andhra Pradesh Legislative Assembly adjourned sine die
content: House met for 15 days and conducted proceedings for 72 hours and 14 minutes, says Speaker Ayyanna Patrudu

title: Velur village to launch protest against removal of ‘St. Thomas’ from 103-year-old school’s name
content: Name changed after  school management handed it over to State government a few years ago. Demand to restore original name had been submitted to government and representations had been sent to Education Minister. Despite these efforts, no response was received from authorities so far

title: Learn new skills to thrive amid AI driven job shifts: JNTU GV Vice-Chancellor
content: Students must focus on emerging technology courses and industry relevant skills as recruitment strategies evolve in the era of Artificial Intelligence, he says

title: A.P.’s revenue deficit exceeded FRBM target of 2.7% in 2024-25, says report
content: Fiscal deficit reamined at 5.05% against the mandated 4% ceiling, driving total borrowings to ₹81,071 crore, crowding out capital expenditure, worsening debt sustainability, says Accounts at a Glance for 2024-25 prepared by the Principal Accountant General of A.P.

title: Kerala govt. publishes terms of reference of 12th pay panel
content: The ToR requires the commission to suggest measures to make the Civil Services more people-friendly and efficient and examine the “multi-faceted” issues faced by women employees

title: ‘Welcome to Kerala’ signboard sparks tension in the Nilgiris
content: An official said residents and political outfits raised objections after officials of Kerala’s Public Works Department placed the board at Thaloor on the evening of Thursday (March 5, 2026)

title: Puthuyuga Yatra to conclude in Thiruvananthapuram on Saturday
content: Rahul Gandhi, Leader of the Opposition in the Lok Sabha, will address a public meeting in this connection

title: Focus on infrastructure, questions over AB-ArK package revision
content: Chief Minister Siddaramaiah announced a ₹900-crore grant for infrastructure development in the health sector.

title: Trump plans 'all-encompassing' college sports EO
content: President Donald Trump on Friday said he would write an executive order within a week that would "solve all of the problems" brought forth in an unprecedented meeting at the White House to address the future of college sports.

title: Source: Patriots trading center Bradbury to Bears
content: The Bears found a new starting center, acquiring Garrett Bradbury from the Patriots in exchange for a 2027 fifth-round pick, a source told ESPN.

title: Fired Michigan coach Moore reaches plea deal
content: Fired Michigan football coach Sherrone Moore pleaded no contest to two misdemeanors to resolve a felony home invasion case. The deal was struck on the same day that a judge planned to hear a challenge to Moore's arrest in December.

title: Penguins' Malkin banned 5 games for Dahlin slash
content: Pittsburgh forward Evgeni Malkin has been suspended five games for slashing Buffalo defenseman Rasmus Dahlin during Thursday's game between the Penguins and Sabres.

title: Texans grant Mixon release after lost '25 season
content: The Texans released veteran running back Joe Mixon on Friday after he missed the 2025 season with a foot injury.

title: Stewart: Union letter not to undermine Jackson
content: WNBPA vice president Breanna Stewart said Friday that the letter to players' union executive director Terri Jackson earlier this week was intended to "get the entire [executive committee] back on track" to get the best CBA possible.

title: UNC's Wilson (thumb surgery) done for season
content: North Carolina star freshman Caleb Wilson will miss the remainder of the season after breaking his right thumb during practice, the school announced.

title: Avalanche bring Kadri back in swap with Flames
content: The Avalanche have re-acquired forward Nazem Kadri in a trade with the Flames. Kadri won a Stanley Cup with Colorado in the 2021-22 season but left for Calgary as a free agent that offseason.

title: ✍️ NHL trade deadline winners and losers
content: The final week was a fun one, with some late shockers. Here's who feels great now -- and who might not.

title: Men's Bracketology: Rating Miami (Ohio) and other potential bid thieves
content: Last year, we had no bid thieves -- but as Champ Week gets underway, plenty of teams look vulnerable.

title: Men's Bubble Watch: Iowa still looks tourney-bound; streaking USF needs some help
content: The Hawkeyes nearly pulled an upset, solidifying their slot. USF keeps winning, but that might not be enough.

title: 2026 NFL free agency: Biggest needs, predictions for 32 teams
content: Malik Willis, Tyler Linderbaum, Mike Evans and Jaelan Phillips are worth keeping an eye on when free agency opens March 11.

title: Projecting contracts for 20 free agents: Can Alec Pierce earn north of $25M per year?
content: Dan Graziano made calls around the NFL to get a sense of how contracts might look for 20 free agents.

title: World Baseball Classic predictions: Will Japan repeat or is United States the team to beat?
content: Our experts make their picks for every round of the 2026 WBC -- from pool play through the championship game.

title: All the best handshakes and celebrations
content: Though players have less time with their international teammates, chemistry at the World Baseball Classic is high -- as evidenced by some of the handshakes this March.

title: Women's Bracketology: Big wins for BYU, Arizona State push them right to the bubble
content: Two teams making the most of Champ Week? BYU and Arizona State, which now both sit just beyond the 68.

title: 'Baby Jokic': What makes Cameron Boozer unstoppable in his pursuit of greatness
content: The star freshman has led teams to championships throughout his basketball career. Can he do it again for Duke?

title: Can the old Liverpool resurface again? Salah and Robertson suggest so in FA Cup
content: Liverpool's aggressive win over Wolves in the FA Cup shows that Liverpool can still turn back the clock, despite their recent struggles.

title: Six points, 14-second running clock: Iowa HS sophomore clinches state championship berth
content: Dallas Center-Grimes' Ava Smid caught fire at the game's conclusion to avoid elimination and punch their ticket to the state title game.

title: 'Right now, I could be UFC champion': Bella Mir is the future face of fighting
content: With Dana White as her mentor, the daughter of heavyweight champ Frank grapples for an NCAA title this weekend.

title: Barnwell: Sizing up the DJ Moore, Trent McDuffie blockbuster trades from all angles
content: The NFL's trade market has been active, with the Bills dealing for DJ Moore and the Rams adding Trent McDuffie. What do the moves mean for all four teams?

title: The craziest F1 start ever? Why Australian Grand Prix might see chaos from the start
content: Grand prix starts have always held the potential for chaos, but this year there's almost guaranteed drama when the lights go out in Australia on Sunday. ESPN explains why.

title: How a 21-year-old prodigy and a 38-year-old veteran ended up with the same fight
content: UFC 326 features a fight between Raul Rosas Jr. and Rob Font in an ultimate battle of youth vs. experience.

title: A look at MVP's fights on ESPN: Can Baumgardner become the face of women's boxing?
content: Andreas Hale and Nick Parkinson offer their take on MVP's newly announced bouts and the implications for the contenders and champions involved.

title: Mike Clay's rookie rankings for 2026: Jeremiyah Love on top
content: With the NFL draft fast approaching, Mike Clay provides his rankings and analysis of 80 of the top skill position players from the combine and what to expect in fantasy.

title: 2026 MLB betting guide: Our favorite prop bet for every team
content: Looking for a prop bet on your favorite team? Our experts break down the standouts for all 30 MLB teams.

title: Seven U.S. Paralympians to watch at the Milan Cortina Games
content: From 36-year-old legend Oksana Masters to 16-year-old Meg Gustafson, here's who to know as the Paralympics begin.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Madrid eye moves for Rodri, Schlotterbeck
content: Real Madrid are looking to the Premier League and Bundesliga to reinforce in the summer. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: LPG price hike: Domestic, commercial cylinders to get costlier from today — report
content: Domestic cooking gas prices are set to rise from Saturday, with a 14.2-kg LPG cylinder increasing by Rs 60 nationwide. Commercial cylinders will also see a Rs 115 hike. This marks the first significant domestic LPG price change since April 2025. Despite global energy concerns, the government assures comfortable energy supply and diversified import sources.

title: Mic-drop mandate as Nepal grooves to rapper Balen’s beat
content: Nepal's political landscape has been dramatically reshaped as the Rastriya Swatantra Party, spearheaded by Balen Shah, achieved a stunning victory in the parliamentary elections. This generational shift has seen veteran leaders facing significant defeats, with the urban electorate overwhelmingly backing the new force. The results signal a powerful rejection of the old political order.

title: 'Me bas fielding pe cricket nahi khela': Amir launches brutal attack on Kaif
content: Mohammad Amir and Mohammad Kaif are engaged in a verbal spat during the T20 World Cup 2026. Kaif accused Amir of seeking attention by criticizing India, while Amir retorted by questioning Kaif's career stats and suggesting he owes his success to Sourav Ganguly. Amir clarified his comments were opinions, not personal attacks.

title: South Delhi home horror: Woman 'kills' 2 daughters, attempts suicide
content: In a shocking Malviya Nagar incident, police found two sisters dead and their mother unconscious. Authorities suspect the mother, Sunita, killed her daughters, Radhika and Gunisha, before attempting suicide. Crushed naphthalene balls and a crepe bandage were recovered from the scene. Investigations into potential depression and domestic discord are ongoing.

title: Microsoft becomes first company to say it is not 'abandoning' Anthropic
content: Microsoft will continue to integrate Anthropic's AI models into its products, despite the Department of War designating the startup as a supply-chain risk. The company's legal team reviewed the designation and concluded Anthropic's offerings, excluding those for the Department of War, can remain available to customers.

title: 'Lost to Australia in 2023': Akhtar says Ahmedabad pressure will haunt India in T20 WC final
content: Former Pakistan cricketers Shoaib Akhtar and Umar Gul highlighted the immense pressure on India ahead of the T20 World Cup 2026 final against New Zealand.  They cited the weight of fan expectations and India's recent struggles at the Narendra Modi Stadium, including losses in major finals, as potential challenges for the home team.

title: 'Headless batting in semi-final': R Ashwin’s brutal verdict on Abhishek Sharma
content: Ravichandran Ashwin backs young opener Abhishek Sharma for the T20 World Cup final despite his struggles, urging the team to persist with him. Ashwin, however, advised Abhishek to better understand match situations and avoid 'headless batting,' emphasizing timing over brute force. He stressed keeping the talented opener in a good mental space for the crucial clash.

title: JP Morgan CEO does not agree with 'work from home', says 4-day-week possible with AI
content: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon, previously skeptical of remote work, now sees AI as a potential game-changer, predicting it could shorten the work week to four days within decades. While acknowledging AI's capacity to boost productivity and improve work-life balance, Dimon also cautioned about potential job disruptions and urged proactive preparation for labor market shifts.

title: Nitish Kumar firm on Rajya Sabha move, son Nishant to join JDU tomorrow
content: Nishant Kumar, son of Bihar CM Nitish Kumar, is set to join the JDU on March 8, marking his political debut. Nitish Kumar has affirmed his decision to move to the Rajya Sabha is final and irreversible, assuring party members he will remain involved in Bihar's development. Nishant's entry sparks speculation about his future role within the party or government.

title: 'Red corridor' will be crushed by March 31, says Amit Shah
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah announced that left-wing extremism will be completely wiped out by March 31, ending the ambition of a "red corridor" from Tirupati to Pashupati. Speaking at CISF's raising day, he lauded security forces for curbing Naxal activities and launched housing projects for the paramilitary force.

title: Oracle layoffs may go up to 30,000 as company faces cash crunch to meet OpenAI commitment
content: Oracle is reportedly planning significant job cuts, potentially thousands, to manage a growing cash crunch fueled by its massive AI data center expansion. This restructuring, costing over $1.6 billion, aims to address financial pressures from a $300 billion OpenAI partnership. The company's stock has seen a substantial decline as Wall Street scrutinizes its aggressive spending and debt.

title: US says India’s Russian oil waiver short-term step to stabilise global prices
content: The US has granted India a temporary waiver to buy Russian oil, aiming to stabilize global prices amid Middle East supply disruptions. This short-term measure allows India to access millions of barrels of Russian crude currently at sea, easing immediate pressure on refineries worldwide.

title: West Bengal: Didi begins sit-in, alleges EC 'plot' to delete voters' names
content: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee launched a sit-in protest, accusing the BJP and Election Commission of a conspiracy to disenfranchise voters by removing genuine names from electoral rolls. She highlighted instances of living individuals being declared dead, vowing to expose the alleged plot ahead of the EC's visit and upcoming assembly polls.

title: 'Jana Nayagan' may arrive in theatres on this date
content: Thalapathy Vijay's 'Jana Nayagan' is nearing its censor certification, with a screening scheduled for March 9, 2026. This development could pave the way for a release, potentially before the Tamil Nadu elections in late April or early May. The film's release was initially planned for Pongal but was delayed.

title: Fans slam censored Lionel Messi remarks after Donald Trump White House meet
content: Lionel Messi met U.S. President Donald Trump during a White House ceremony honoring Inter Miami’s 2025 MLS Cup victory. Messi gifted Trump a signed pink soccer ball, while the president praised his impact on the team. Photos from the event quickly spread online, where fans shared mixed reactions. The visit followed a long tradition of championship teams celebrating at the White House.

title: Olympic champion Alysa Liu makes urgent plea after frightening airport incident
content: Olympic champion Alysa Liu asked fans to respect her privacy after a frightening airport incident following her Milan Winter Olympics victory. The 20-year-old figure skater said people crowded her and chased her to her car. Liu, who recently ended a four-year retirement to win gold, says she hopes to keep her life calm despite sudden fame.

title: Rory McIlroy backs Scottie Scheffler stance, sparks Tom Brady debate
content: Since the 2024 Masters, Scottie Scheffler has been expressive about golf being just a small part of his life. Rory McIlroy also added, “I think that works in his favor, and he has people around him that keep him incredibly grounded.” According to Tom Brady in the latest edition of his newsletter, before the 2024 Masters, Scheffler had already stated his position.

title: IAF pilot part of Operation Sindoor among 2 killed in Assam Sukhoi crash
content: Two IAF pilots, Squadron Leader Anuj Vashishth and Flight Lieutenant Purvesh Duragkar, were killed when their Su-30MKI jet crashed in Assam's Karbi Anglong district during a training sortie. The aircraft had taken off from Jorhat airbase and lost contact shortly after. Duragkar was known for his participation in Operation Sindoor last year.

title: MBBS graduate tops civil services exam in which professionals dominate
content: AIIMS graduate Anuj Agnihotri has clinched the top spot in the Civil Services Examination 2025, with 958 candidates recommended for various services. Medical, engineering, law, and commerce graduates once again dominated the prestigious exam. Rajeshwari Suve secured the second rank, followed by Ekansh Dhull at third.

title: Karnataka says it will ban social media for children under 16
content: Karnataka plans to ban social media for children under 16, aligning with global efforts to curb digital exposure. Chief Minister Siddaramaiah announced the policy, aiming to address concerns over screen addiction and mental health. While welcomed by educators, experts caution about potential FOMO and withdrawal, urging careful implementation and support for children.

title: Middle East conflict: There should be no cherry-picking on int'l law, says Iran dy FM
content: India's External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar met with Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh on the sidelines of Raisina Dialogue 2026. The meeting followed India's condolences over the killing of Iran's supreme leader. Khatibzadeh emphasized the importance of upholding international law against perceived atrocities and aggression.

title: Opposition notice to oust Speaker Om Birla to be taken up in Lok Sabha on Monday
content: Lok Sabha will debate a no-confidence motion against Speaker Om Birla on March 9, moved by Congress and allies over alleged partisan conduct. Despite NDA's majority rendering the motion's defeat inevitable, it will test opposition unity. Whips have been issued by both BJP and Congress for the crucial vote.

title: Swift, Kelce's relationship faces an unexpected complication as their wedding nears
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s rumored wedding plans may face unexpected attention as another high-profile celebrity wedding is scheduled around the same time. Sources claim the overlap could reignite discussions about Swift’s past relationship, potentially drawing comparisons online. The pop star reportedly hopes her big day remains focused on her relationship with Kelce rather than past headlines resurfacing.

title: Self-reliance only way to stay relevant in era of global uncertainty: Rajnath Singh
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh emphasized self-reliance as crucial for navigating global uncertainty, citing supply chain realignments and rising maritime activities. He highlighted India's growing domestic defence production, which surpassed Rs 1.5 lakh crore in FY24-25, with exports reaching Rs 24,000 crore. Singh also stressed the importance of technological dynamism and private sector participation in achieving defence 'atmanirbharta'.

title: Jailed as juvenile, Bangladesh man freed after 21 years in jail
content: A Bangladeshi national walks free after 21 years in jail for murder. The Calcutta High Court ruled he was a minor when the crime occurred. Another convict from Birbhum district also gained freedom after 14 years on similar grounds. The court emphasized juvenile justice laws.

title: Cross, Jesus statue at home not proof of conversion, rules high court
content: The Bombay High Court ruled that religious symbols in a home do not prove conversion to Christianity, emphasizing the need for documentary evidence like baptism certificates. The court set aside a caste scrutiny committee's decision, which had denied a Scheduled Caste certificate based on such inferences, and directed its issuance.

title: Mahila Jan Sunwais: NCW plans week-long public hearings covering 500 districts
content: The National Commission for Women is launching nationwide 'Mahila Jan Sunwais' in 500 districts across 25 states until March 14. This initiative aims to resolve approximately 15,000 complaints, with a focus on domestic violence and dowry harassment. NCW chief Vijaya Rahatkar will lead hearings in five states lacking commission chairpersons, bringing support directly to women in distress.

title: UK to offer up to £40,000 to failed asylum-seekers to leave country
content: Britain is piloting a scheme offering up to £40,000 per family to failed asylum-seekers to leave voluntarily. Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood stated this aims to save taxpayer money, as hotel accommodation costs are substantial. Families have seven days to decide, or face forced removal. The government is also making refugee status temporary and introducing new legal routes.

title: India gave safe harbour to another Iranian ship days before IRIS Dena was sunk by US
content: Just days before the Iranian warship IRIS Dena was sunk by a US strike, India provided urgent assistance to another Iranian vessel, IRIS Lavan.  The ship, facing technical issues, was granted emergency docking at Kochi port on March 1st and arrived on March 4th. Its 183 crew members are currently housed at Indian naval facilities.

title: White House glorifies Iran strike with Hollywood video, sparks backlash
content: The White House posted a 42-second, trailer-style montage celebrating US–Israeli strikes on Iran, blending real drone footage with Hollywood, anime, and video-game clips. Phrases like “Justice the American Way” frame war as spectacle, drawing viral attention and criticism. Observers condemned the surreal, propagandistic tone, noting copyright concerns, ironic film choices, and the ethical risk of trivializing deadly military operations.

title: 'Look in the mirror': Ex-England pacer questions Buttler’s future after T20 World Cup flop
content: Former England pacer Steve Harmison has cast doubt on Jos Buttler's continued motivation for international cricket following a disappointing T20 World Cup 2026 campaign. Buttler struggled significantly, scoring only 87 runs and failing to register a half-century. Harmison urged Buttler to self-reflect on his desire to continue, suggesting it might be time for a new generation if his passion wanes.

title: Vinnie Hinostroza Trade: Florida Panthers reunite with forward ahead of NHL trade deadline
content: The Florida Panthers acquired forward Vinnie Hinostroza from the Minnesota Wild just hours before the NHL Trade Deadline. The Panthers' general manager, Bill Zito, signed Hinostroza in 2020, which makes this trade a reunion for the two players. The experienced forward will help Florida during its important season-ending matches because he provides essential depth to its roster.

title: Nepal elections results 2026: Full list of winners
content: Nepal's Gen-Z uprising has yielded early results in parliamentary polls, with the Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) making significant inroads. The RSP has secured key seats in Kathmandu and is leading in over 110 constituencies nationwide. Notably, RSP leader Balendra Shah is ahead of former PM KP Sharma Oli in Jhapa-5.

title: Rory McIlroy reveals how Roger Federer helped him rediscover motivation
content: After completing the Career Grand Slam with victory at The Masters, Rory McIlroy turned to tennis great Roger Federer, whose advice about surpassing Pete Sampras’ record helped McIlroy rethink his goals and find renewed motivation. The golfer was impressed with Federer's answer which he thought was great as he’s lived it and he’s been through it.

title: Vehicles torched, roads blocked: Protest in Delhi after man killed in Holi clash
content: Protests erupted in southwest Delhi's Uttam Nagar after a 26-year-old man died following a Holi clash. Tarun succumbed to injuries after a dispute over coloured water escalated into a physical fight. Demonstrators blocked a major road, set vehicles ablaze, and demanded strict action against the accused.

title: T20 WC final: Should India persist with 'weak link' Abhishek or bring in Rinku
content: As India gear up for the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 final against New Zealand, a debate rages over opener Abhishek Sharma's place. Despite low scores, some back him as an 'X factor', while others advocate for Rinku Singh's inclusion, citing predictability. The team management faces a crucial decision on continuity versus a late change.

title: 'Where’s the rest of you?': McCullum stunned by Rohit Sharma’s fitness - Watch
content: Former India captain Rohit Sharma's striking physical transformation drew admiration from England's Eoin Morgan and Brendon McCullum at the Wankhede Stadium.  Sharma, present as a guest, engaged warmly with players from both sides, offering encouragement and insights before India's crucial T20 World Cup semi-final victory. His presence added to the pre-match buzz.

title: Nazi salute costs Real Madrid as UEFA slaps $17,000 fine after fan's gesture
content: UEFA has fined Real Madrid 15,000 euros and ordered a partial stadium closure. This action follows a supporter's Nazi salute before a Champions League match. The club identified and removed the individual. Real Madrid condemned the gesture. The incident occurred before their match against Benfica. The Spanish club will face Manchester City next.

title: Notice to remove Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla will be taken up on March 9
content: Lok Sabha will debate a rare motion to remove Speaker Om Birla on Monday, with 118 opposition MPs backing the notice. Allegations include partisan conduct and silencing dissent. The resolution requires 50 MPs to stand in support to proceed. Both BJP and Congress have issued whips for MPs to attend.

title: US Pressures Sri Lanka Not To Repatriate Survivors Of Sunk Iran Ship: Report
content: The United States is pressing Sri Lanka's government not to repatriate the survivors from the Iranian warship it sank this week, as well as the crew of a second Iranian ship that is in Sri Lankan custody

title: Nepal Election 2026 Results Live Updates: Rapper-Politician Balen Shah's RSP Wins 20 Seats, Leads In Over 95
content: Nepal General Election 2026 Results Live: Rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah's newly formed Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) was on Friday heading towards a sweeping victory in Nepal's first general elections since the violent Gen Z protests

title: Iran's Ballistic Missiles Could Be Under 1,000, Drone Attacks Tapering: Ian Bremmer To NDTV
content: The crucial point about the war in Iran -- how long it will carry on -- is not something anyone wants to commit to, but Ian Bremmer, president of the Eurasia Group, said it is certain that Iran is losing steam in terms of ammunition after just seven

title: US Says Operation Against Iran To Last 4 To 6 Weeks
content: As the conflict in West Asia further intensifies, the White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt told media on Friday that the achievable objectives of Operation Epic Fury are expected to last about four to six weeks.

title: Domestic LPG Price Hiked By Rs 60, Commercial Cylinder Up Rs 115 Amid Iran War
content: The price of domestic LPG cylinders has been increased with effect from Saturday, March 7, with the cost of a 14.2-kg household cooking gas cylinder rising by Rs 60 across the country, according to sources.

title: Qatar Announces Partial Redemption Of Airspace Amid Iran War
content: Qatar's Civil Aviation Authority announced on Friday the partial resumption of air navigation in the country via designated navigational contingency routes with limited operational capacity.

title: US Suspects China In Breach Of FBI Surveillance Network: Report
content: US investigators believe hackers affiliated with the Chinese government are responsible for a cyber intrusion on an internal Federal Bureau of Investigation computer network that holds information related to some domestic surveillance orders.

title: US, Israel-Iran War News Live Updates: US Oil Price Soars Over 10% After Trump Demand On Iran
content: The raging war in the Middle East entered its seventh day on Friday, with the Israeli military announcing a "next phase" in the conflict and bombing Lebanon's Beirut.

title: Pak Man Found Guilty Of Plot To Kill Trump, US Officials
content: A Pakistani man with alleged ties to Iran was found guilty Friday of plotting to assassinate Donald Trump or other US officials in retaliation for the American military killing of Revolutionary Guards commander Qassem Soleimani, prosecutors said.

title: Security Tightened In Delhi After Man Killed In Holi Clash; 4 Arrested
content: Security has been tightened in the Uttam Nagar area of Delhi after a 25-year-old man succumbed to injuries he sustained during a fight that broke out between neighbours from different communities during Holi celebrations earlier this week.

title: Mamata Banerjee Launches Protest Against Bengal Voter Roll Deletions
content: In a dramatic return to the street politics that once defined her rise, West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee on Friday launched a dharna in central Kolkata against large-scale deletions in the state's voter rolls.

title: Noida International Airport Gets Key Licence, To Begin Operations Soon
content: The Directorate General of Civil Aviation on Friday issued the aerodrome licence to the Noida International Airport in Jewar, marking a key step towards the commencement of flight operations, officials said.

title: 'Reached Out To India To Buy Russian Oil To Ease Global Markets': US
content: The United States has "reached out to India" to purchase Russian oil currently sitting in floating storage in southern Asia as part of a short-term effort to ease pressure on global oil markets, US Energy Secretary Chris Wright said.

title: US Loses 92,000 Jobs In February As Unemployment Rate Ticked Up To 4.4%
content: American employers unexpectedly cut 92,000 jobs last month, a sign that the labor market remains under strain. The unemployment rate blipped up to 4.4%.

title: Evidence Suggests Deadly Blast At Iranian School Was Likely A US Airstrike
content: Satellite images, expert analysis, a US official and public information released by the US and Israeli militaries suggest an explosion that killed scores of Iranian students at a school was likely caused by US airstrikes.

title: 'Trump, Netanyahu Greatest Tag Team In History': Israel PM's Adviser To NDTV
content: Ophir Falk, the foreign policy adviser to Benjamin Netanyahu, said Israel and the US do not want an endless war with Iran.

title: US Pressing Sri Lanka Not To Repatriate Iranian Ship's Crew: Report
content: The United States is pressing Sri Lanka's government not to repatriate the survivors from the Iranian warship it sank this week, as well as the crew of a second Iranian ship that is in Sri Lankan custody, according to reports.

title: Drugs Worth Rs 10 Crore Seized As Cops Bust Network Linking Nepal, Lanka
content: The Narcotics Control Bureau (NCB) has busted an international drug trafficking network operating between Nepal, India and Sri Lanka, seizing narcotics worth around Rs 10 crore and arresting five people.

title: Rajasthan Passes Bill Restricting Property Transfers In 'Disturbed Areas'
content: The Rajasthan assembly, on Wednesday, passed a divisive bill that would regulate property transfers in communally sensitive regions.

title: Russia Backing Iran In War With US-Israel By Sharing Military Intel: Report
content: Russia has entered the war between the US-Israel alliance and Iran, although indirectly. Russia is providing its strategic ally Iran with intelligence to help attack US warships and aircraft in the Middle East.

title: 'We voted for you, now do your job': Nepal’s Gen Z warn new leaders
content: the historic verdict came with a clear call for the country's new leaders: 'we voted for you. Now get to work' in Kathmandu, there were no dhols or gulal left over from Holi to be sprayed in celebration . the generation that drove the uprising said the "old parties had been punished" but the deeper issues that brought them onto the streets remained unresolved .

title: Lamichhane: TV star who built RSP that caught Nepal’s imagination in just 4 years
content: he launched the Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) just before the 2022 election . he offered a simple promise: the old parties had failed and a cleaner alternative had arrived . in the first parliamentary election since the Gen Z uprising, that promise found its biggest audience yet .
